titlestmt.titleproper: Register to The Schneeloch Family Papers
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Dorothée Recordon

conventiondeclaration.abbr: Finding aid prepared according to local Music Library descriptive practices.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday November 8, 2018 at 01:34

Transformed with yale.addEadidUrl.xsl. Adds @url with handle for finding aid. Overwrites @url if already present.

did.abstract: Diaries, correspondence, other materials by and about the American singers Emma Waleska Schneeloch Bacon (1862-1925) and Emilie Auguste Schneeloch Busse (b. 1868)

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open to researchers by appointment. There are no restricted materials in the collection. Please contact the Special Collections staff to schedule an appointment. Some of the materials may be stored at the Library's off-campus shelving facility, so researchers should allow at least two business days to have the appropriate boxes paged.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Schneeloch Family Papers are the physical property of the Irving S. Gilmore Music Library of Yale University. Copyrights belong to the composers and authors, or their legal heirs and assigns.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: MSS 6, The Schneeloch Family Papers in the Irving S. Gilmore Music Library of Yale University.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: In 5 series as follows: I. Correspondence. II. Printed matter. III. Miscellaneous. IV. Photographs. V. Addendum.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Schneeloch Family Papers document the careers of Emma Schneeloch Bacon and Emilie Schneeloch Busse. The Papers include diaries, letters, programs, photographs, and newspaper clippings.
Additional information about the Schneeloch Family can be found in the Bacon-Schneeloch Family Papers, located in the Manuscripts and Archives Division of Yale University's Sterling Memorial Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Emma Waleska Schneeloch Bacon (1862-1925) and Emilie Auguste Schneeloch Busse (b. 1868) were sisters from New Haven, Connecticut who became popular singers. Emma was a contralto, and Emilie a soprano. They frequently sang duets, but they were best known as members of a vocal quartet associated with Patrick Gilmore's band. The Schneeloch sisters made two national concert tours with Gilmore's band.

did.unittitle: Diary, Emilie Schneeloch's of Western Trip with Gilmore's band
Diary, Emilie Schneeloch's of Western Trip with Gilmore's band

odd.head: General
odd.p: Newspaper clipping, Sep 5, 1965 New York Times, Sunday, about Patrick Sarsfield Gilmore

did.unittitle: Frank Busse and his wife, Millie Schneeloch Busse
Gilmore's Great Quartette : P.S. Gilmore, Emma and Emilie Schneeloch, Myron W. Whitney, Signor Victor Clodio
1 pamphlet and notes on performing techniques
